Headline: Financial - Private Special Schools, National
Info: An investigation has revealed that councils are facing unsustainable financial pressures, spending up to four times as much per pupil on private special schools compared to state equivalents. In response, the government is preparing to implement new funding bands to rein in these escalating costs.

Headline: Financial - Settle College (State), North Yorkshire
Info: A controversial home-to-school transport policy is contributing to severe financial pressures and falling pupil numbers at Settle College. Local councillors have raised concerns about the state school's future viability if the demographic and financial impacts continue.

Headline: Merger - Beaudesert Park School, Gloucestershire
Info: Beaudesert Park School, a highly regarded independent prep school, has announced it will merge with the historic Rugby School Group from July 2026, benefiting from a seven-figure investment in its site and facilities [6.17].

Headline: Leadership - The Education Alliance (State), East Yorkshire
Info: Jonny Uttley has announced he is stepping down as Chief Executive of The Education Alliance, a multi-academy trust comprising 12 schools, after six years in the role. Scott Ratheram will serve as interim CEO while a recruitment process takes place.

Headline: Merger - Tilery Primary School (State), Stockton
Info: Tilery Primary School has officially joined the Northern Lights Learning Trust, becoming the twelfth school to merge into the growing multi-academy trust.

Headline: Leadership - David Ross Education Trust (State), National
Info: The David Ross Education Trust has named Simon Rose as its new interim Chief Executive Officer following a rethink of the trust's leadership structure.

Headline: Financial - Private School Sector, Wiltshire
Info: A new survey by wealth management firm Saltus reveals that nearly a tenth of wealthy parents have withdrawn their children from the independent sector since the introduction of VAT on school fees. Local MPs have highlighted recent closures, such as Rendcomb College and Bishopstrow College, as direct consequences of these financial pressures.

Headline: Closure - Manorbier Church in Wales VC School (State), Pembrokeshire
Info: Pembrokeshire County Council has voted to proceed with the statutory closure of Manorbier Church in Wales VC School, which was destroyed by fire in 2022. The council concluded that rebuilding the state school would not represent value for money due to low pupil numbers in the area.

Headline: Closure - Woodcote House, Surrey
Info: A newly published retrospective highlights the permanent closure of the 150-year-old independent preparatory school, underscoring a broader trend that saw 45 prep schools close over the past year due to severe financial headwinds and VAT policies.

Headline: Closure - Shaugh Prior Primary School (State), Devon
Info: Devon County Council has issued a statutory proposal to permanently close the rural primary school, which currently has only two pupils, due to long-term educational and financial unsustainability.

Headline: Closure - Kings Education Beckenham, London
Info: Kings Education has announced the permanent closure of its Beckenham campus at the end of the current term, citing external financial pressures and a decline in enrolments exacerbated by the introduction of VAT on private school fees.

Headline: Merger - Independent Schools Sector, England and Wales
Info: The latest Good Merger Index reports a record spike in charity mergers, driven predominantly by the consolidation of the independent school sector as institutions face VAT changes, cost pressures, and declining fee income.

Headline: Financial - Isle of Wight Primary Schools (State), Isle of Wight
Info: A new council report warns that 23 maintained primary schools on the island are forecast to run financial deficits by the 2026/27 academic year, making the reduction of school places and potential closures a critical priority.
